NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. In the LORD I put my trust; How can you say to my soul, “Flee as a bird to your mountain”?
NKJV.2: For look! The wicked bend their bow, They make ready their arrow on the string, That they may shoot secretly at the upright in heart.
NKJV.3: If the foundations are destroyed, What can the righteous do?
NKJV.4: The LORD is in His holy temple, The LORD’s throne is in heaven; His eyes behold, His eyelids test the sons of men.
NKJV.5: The LORD tests the righteous, But the wicked and the one who loves violence His soul hates.
NKJV.6: Upon the wicked He will rain coals; Fire and brimstone and a burning wind Shall be the portion of their cup.
NKJV.7: For the LORD is righteous, He loves righteousness; His countenance beholds the upright.

ESV.1: In the LORD I take refuge; how can you say to my soul, “Flee like a bird to your mountain,
ESV.2: for behold, the wicked bend the bow; they have fitted their arrow to the string to shoot in the dark at the upright in heart;
ESV.3: if the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous do?”
ESV.4: The LORD is in his holy temple; the LORD's throne is in heaven; his eyes see, his eyelids test the children of man.
ESV.5: The LORD tests the righteous, but his soul hates the wicked and the one who loves violence.
ESV.6: Let him rain coals on the wicked; fire and sulfur and a scorching wind shall be the portion of their cup.
ESV.7: For the LORD is righteous; he loves righteous deeds; the upright shall behold his face.

NASB.1: For the choir director. [A Psalm] of David. In the LORD I take refuge; How can you say to my soul, 'Flee [as] a bird to your mountain;
NASB.2: For, behold, the wicked bend the bow, They make ready their arrow upon the string To shoot in darkness at the upright in heart.
NASB.3: If the foundations are destroyed, What can the righteous do?'
NASB.4: The LORD is in His holy temple; the LORD'S throne is in heaven; His eyes behold, His eyelids test the sons of men.
NASB.5: The LORD tests the righteous and the wicked, And the one who loves violence His soul hates.
NASB.6: Upon the wicked He will rain snares; Fire and brimstone and burning wind will be the portion of their cup.
NASB.7: For the LORD is righteous, He loves righteousness; The upright will behold His face.

NLT.1:
I trust in the LORD for protection.
So why do you say to me,
“Fly like a bird to the mountains for safety!
NLT.2:
The wicked are stringing their bows
and fitting their arrows on the bowstrings.
They shoot from the shadows
at those whose hearts are right.
NLT.3:
The foundations of law and order have collapsed.
What can the righteous do?”
NLT.4:
But the LORD is in his holy Temple;
the LORD still rules from heaven.
He watches everyone closely,
examining every person on earth.
NLT.5:
The LORD examines both the righteous and the wicked.
He hates those who love violence.
NLT.6:
He will rain down blazing coals and burning sulfur on the wicked,
punishing them with scorching winds.
NLT.7:
For the righteous LORD loves justice.
The virtuous will see his face.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. By David. In Yahweh, I take refuge. How can you say to my soul, “Flee as a bird to your mountain!”
WEB.2: For, behold, the wicked bend their bows. They set their arrows on the strings, that they may shoot in darkness at the upright in heart.
WEB.3: If the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous do?
WEB.4: Yahweh is in his holy temple. Yahweh is on his throne in heaven. His eyes observe. His eyes examine the children of men.
WEB.5: Yahweh examines the righteous, but the wicked and him who loves violence his soul hates.
WEB.6: On the wicked he will rain blazing coals; fire, sulfur, and scorching wind shall be the portion of their cup.
WEB.7: For Yahweh is righteous. He loves righteousness. The upright shall see his face.

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. In the LORD I take refuge [and put my trust];
How can you say to me, "Flee like a bird to your mountain;
AMP.2:
For look, the wicked are bending the bow;
They take aim with their arrow on the string
To shoot [by stealth] in darkness at the upright in heart.
AMP.3:
"If the foundations [of a godly society] are destroyed,
What can the righteous do?"
AMP.4:
The LORD is in His holy temple; the Lord's throne is in heaven.
His eyes see, His eyelids test the children of men.
AMP.5:
The LORD tests the righteous and the wicked,
And His soul hates the [malevolent] one who loves violence.
AMP.6:
Upon the wicked (godless) He will rain coals of fire;
Fire and brimstone and a dreadful scorching wind will be the portion of their cup [of doom].
AMP.7:
For the LORD is [absolutely] righteous, He loves righteousness (virtue, morality, justice);
The upright shall see His face.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
I've already run for dear life
straight to the arms of God.
So why would I run away now
when you say,
"Run to the mountains;
MSG.2:
the evil
bows are bent, the wicked arrows
Aimed to shoot under cover of darkness
at every heart open to God.
MSG.3:
The bottom's dropped out of the country;
good people don't have a chance"?
MSG.4:
But God hasn't moved to the mountains;
his holy address hasn't changed.
He's in charge, as always, his eyes
taking everything in, his eyelids
Unblinking, examining Adam's unruly brood
inside and out, not missing a thing.
MSG.5:
He tests the good and the bad alike;
if anyone cheats, God's outraged.
MSG.6:
Fail the test and you're out,
out in a hail of firestones,
Drinking from a canteen
filled with hot desert wind.
MSG.7:
God's business is putting things right;
he loves getting the lines straight,
Setting us straight. Once we're standing tall,
we can look him straight in the eye.

NIV.1:
Psalm 11
For the director of music. Of David.
In the Lord I take refuge.
How then can you say to me:
“Flee like a bird to your mountain.
NIV.2:
For look, the wicked bend their bows;
they set their arrows against the strings
to shoot from the shadows
at the upright in heart.
NIV.3:
When the foundations are being destroyed,
what can the righteous do?”
NIV.4:
The Lord is in his holy temple;
the Lord is on his heavenly throne.
He observes the sons of men;
his eyes examine them.
NIV.5:
The Lord examines the righteous,
but the wicked and those who love violence
his soul hates.
NIV.6:
On the wicked he will rain
fiery coals and burning sulfur;
a scorching wind will be their lot.
NIV.7:
For the Lord is righteous,
he loves justice;
upright men will see his face.

NRSVCE.1:
In the LORD I take refuge; how can you say to me,
“Flee like a bird to the mountains;
NRSVCE.2:
for look, the wicked bend the bow,
they have fitted their arrow to the string,
to shoot in the dark at the upright in heart.
NRSVCE.3:
If the foundations are destroyed,
what can the righteous do?”
NRSVCE.4:
The LORD is in his holy temple;
the LORD’s throne is in heaven.
His eyes behold, his gaze examines humankind.
NRSVCE.5:
The LORD tests the righteous and the wicked,
and his soul hates the lover of violence.
NRSVCE.6:
On the wicked he will rain coals of fire and sulfur;
a scorching wind shall be the portion of their cup.
NRSVCE.7:
For the LORD is righteous;
he loves righteous deeds;
the upright shall behold his face.

NET.1: In the LORD I have taken shelter. How can you say to me, “Flee to a mountain like a bird!
NET.2: For look, the wicked prepare their bows, they put their arrows on the strings, to shoot in the darkness at the morally upright.
NET.3: When the foundations are destroyed, what can the godly accomplish?”
NET.4: The LORD is in his holy temple; the LORD’s throne is in heaven. His eyes watch; his eyes examine all people.
NET.5: The LORD approves of the godly, but he hates the wicked and those who love to do violence.
NET.6: May the LORD rain down burning coals and brimstone on the wicked! A whirlwind is what they deserve!
NET.7: Certainly the LORD is just; he rewards godly deeds; the upright will experience his favor.
